warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The GSFA (Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.parameter: Contaminants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex Alimentarius Commission establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants to be legally permitted in food or feed commodities. These levels are detailed in standards like CXS 193-1995.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: A Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.limit: Verification required against Union list of food additives
rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: The database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.limit: Verification required against food safety criteria and process hygiene criteria
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.limit: Compliance with food safety criteria in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005, verified through shelf-life studies (durability studies and challenge tests)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Technical guidance documents provide information on how to conduct shelf-life studies for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods to comply with food safety criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Technical guidance document on shelf-life studies for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods provides specialised laboratories with detailed and practical information on how to conduct shelf-life studies... in order to comply with the food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

rules.limit: Sampling procedures required on food processing areas and equipment surfaces
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Guidelines describe sampling procedures for food business operators manufacturing RTE food to detect L. monocytogenes on processing surfaces.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Guidelines on sampling the food processing areas and equipment describes sampling procedures to be performed by food business operators manufacturing RTE food which may pose a L. monocytogenes risk for public health in order to detect L. monocytogenes on the surfaces of RTE food processing areas and equipment.

rules.parameter: Compliance with general Halal requirements for food products, including permissible ingredients and additives
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 Halal food - Part 1: General requirements for Halal food
rules.notes: This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for food to be considered Halal, which impacts the selection of ingredients and additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.limit: Use calculation tool for permitted levels
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies if two or more Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid) are used in the food.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies to all food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.limit: Verification required against SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and the type of food products. Applies to all food businesses.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products. Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies to all food sold in Singapore, including seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies to all food products sold in Singapore, including seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of an additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Applies to all food businesses.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Applies to food sold in Singapore, including seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: If laboratory test results indicate contaminants, they must be within specified limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.parameter: Various Contaminants (Microbiological, Heavy Metals, Mycotoxins, Marine Biotoxins, Veterinary Drug Residues, Pesticide Residues)
rules.limit: Verification required against relevant SFA documents
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Various limits are imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. Applies to all food businesses.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards [...] Heavy metals [...] Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins [...] Veterinary drug residues [...] Pesticide residues [...] Other incidental constituents

rules.limit: Verification required against SFA website list
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues in various food commodities.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared on food labels
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Applies to ready-to-eat (RTE) food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.notes: Labeling is recommended if present as an ingredient or food additive.

rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive: ... gluten-containing cereals

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Labeling is recommended if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive: (4) Kiwis and kiwi products thereof

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory labeling required if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive: ... mango

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory labeling required if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive: ... milk and goat milk

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory labeling required if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive: ... peanuts

rules.parameter: Seeds and seed products (e.g., Sunflower seed, melon seed)

rules.notes: Labeling is recommended if present as an ingredient or food additive. Excludes highly refined or purified sunflower seed oil.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive: (3) Seeds and seed products: Sunflower seed, melon seed, etc. However, sunflower seed oil, which is highly refined or purified from sunflower seeds, is not included.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory labeling required if present as an ingredient or food additive.

rules.parameter: Spiral shells and Spiral shell products (e.g., escargot, mussel, clam, oyster, scallop, abalone)

rules.notes: Labeling is recommended if present as an ingredient or food additive.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory labeling required if residue sulphur dioxide is 10 or more mg/kg. Applies to ingredients or food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory labeling required if present as an ingredient or food additive.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Control measures shall be set to prevent cross-contamination. If risk exists, a precautionary statement is suggested.
rules.evidenceQuote: Control measures shall be set in place to prevent food from cross contamination in the production process. If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.limit: Verification required against FDA regulations and inventories
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Manufacturers must verify the regulatory status and approved uses of food and color additives by consulting the Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) and FDA inventories.
rules.evidenceQuote: Approved Food and Color Additives and Petitions Under Review; Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Citations for Color Additives, Food Ingredients and Packaging

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances labeled as 'DELISTED' in the inventory.
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as "DELISTED").

rules.limit: Must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74)

rules.notes: The Substances Added to Food inventory includes these, but verification against the CFR is essential.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.limit: Must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184)

rules.notes: The inventory also includes GRAS substances evaluated by FEMA and JECFA, but FDA regulations are the primary reference for listed GRAS.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. Sesame was added effective January 1, 2023, by the FASTER Act.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. All FDA requirements applicable to major food allergens, including labeling and manufacturing requirements, apply to sesame as of January 1, 2023.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level. Specific limits for Brazil nuts, peanuts, and pistachio nuts are also 20 ppb.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.parameter: Various chemical contaminants (e.g., toxic elements, mycotoxins, persistent pesticides, radionuclides)
rules.limit: Verification required against the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and referenced guidance documents/regulations
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Includes tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Contaminant levels listed in the transparency tool include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels.

rules.limit: Not required by law; should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices have been incorporated and precautions taken. Must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Should not be used as a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes... and must be truthful and not misleading.

rules.limit: Establishments must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per 21 CFR part 117 (CGMP & PC rule).
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Part of the 'Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food' rule.
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances derived from insects.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.limit: Must be declared in one of two ways: in parentheses after the ingredient name, OR in a 'contains' statement immediately after or next to the ingredient list.
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The type of tree nut (e.g., almonds), species of fish (e.g., bass), and species of Crustacean shellfish (e.g., crab) must also be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label in one of two ways. The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient... — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.limit: Must meet a defined standard for gluten content
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling on August 2, 2013, and established compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim on August 12, 2020.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods... bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present in food at or above the specified concentration, due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.sourceTitle: Hệ thống văn bản Chính phủ
